summary: eth-dev@1.0.2 exhibits base64-decoded payload execution patterns consistent with credential/wallet theft
details: The package's index.js contains multiple base64-decoded blobs (Buffer.from(..., "base64") at lines 30, 61, 62, and 98) used to reconstruct hidden strings at runtime. The package name mimics legitimate Ethereum development libraries, and the traced module content matched malware-output patterns severe enough that its detailed analysis was suppressed by the model provider's safety filter — a pattern that co-occurs with operational credential/wallet-stealing code rather than benign minification. The combination of an ethereum-themed lure name, obfuscated base64-decoded runtime strings executed on module load, and content-filtered tracing is consistent with active-attack behavior targeting wallet secrets or environment credentials of the installer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
